sources.label: NZX GNE announcements page (latest item 22 May 2026: FY26 Results Announcement Date; nothing dated Jun 5-9)
Energy-Storage.News - Genesis FID on second-stage Huntly BESS (background, 20 Apr 2026)

verdicts.rankRationale: No NZX announcement or material news since June 6; -1.6% day move and YTD cooling from +7.1% to +5.4% is ordinary drift, so the Scaling-page utility/power-demand case is unchanged from yesterday's hold.
verdicts.bull: Huntly is NZ's swing firming asset and the second-stage 100MW/200MWh BESS FID plus Q3 guidance keep Genesis levered to any North Island data-centre load growth; at $1.7B and only +5% YTD the name is uncrowded with cheap optionality.
verdicts.neutral: A regulated-adjacent gentailer with a Hold consensus near the analyst target (~NZ$2.54); without a signed hyperscale load agreement it tracks NZ power prices and hydrology more than the AI buildout.
verdicts.bear: Coal/gas fuel and carbon costs bite into FY26, NZ data-centre demand may route to Mercury/Contact geothermal instead, and the February equity raise already funded the growth pipeline, capping near-term upside.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: FY26 results (date confirmed 22 May notice) and any firm data-centre or large-load supply agreement tied to Huntly capacity.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Higher fuel/carbon costs and weak NZ demand compress gentailer margins while AI-driven load growth fails to materialize in New Zealand.
